extractedFieldValues.current_value: The patient is considered homebound because they have a symptomatic pathologic fracture of the right fibula with an expansile, infiltrative destructive lesion and soft-tissue extension on MRI, producing moderate tenderness and aching with weightbearing (pain 7/10) that limits safe ambulation and makes exiting the home without assistance unsafe. Objective findings supporting homebound status include: pathologic fracture of the right fibula on plain radiograph with bridging callus, an expansile destructive fibular lesion with soft-tissue extension on MRI concerning for sarcoma, a palpable mass with localized moderate tenderness on exam, and documented pain with weightbearing. These conditions create a risk of further fracture, acute pain exacerbation, and soft-tissue compromise with ambulation; the patient is elderly (77 years) and has comorbid chronic lymphocytic leukemia and is taking antiplatelet therapy (clopidogrel), which increase peri-procedural risk and complicate safe transport and unsupervised activity outside the home. Leaving the home requires a considerable and taxing effort because any weightbearing provokes significant pain, there is structural instability of the fibula due to the pathologic fracture and destructive lesion, and there is no home assistance available to provide safe transfer or supervised mobility. Allowable, limited absences are restricted to medically necessary trips (preoperative evaluation, diagnostic imaging, and planned surgical care) only. Given the need for pre-operative evaluation and coordination, peri-procedural risk management, skilled wound and pain management planning, and close monitoring of a malignant/pathologic fracture, these objective limitations meet Medicare homebound criteria and justify the medical necessity for skilled home health services.
source_spans: Primary Reason for Home Health/Medical Necessity
Patient was not discharged from an inpatient facility
